data.title: Despite recent drawdowns, BTC remains a resilient asset rather than a pure hedge – analyst
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has emerged as a surprisingly resilient asset during the first month of the war in the Middle East, outperforming both gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and technology stocks (VGT), (XLK), (IYW).

The cryptocurrency was up approximately 5% over the past month at the start of Friday—although it has declined 3.8% to $66,173—while gold (XAUUSD:CUR) has fallen around 14.4% and the Nasdaq has dropped roughly 6.5%.

In an interview with CNBC, Gazmararian noted that Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) digital nature gives it unique advantages during periods of geopolitical turmoil.

“A natively digital asset holds a lot of appeal because it can move across borders,” she explained, adding that investors can maintain self-custody of Bitcoin (BTC-USD) rather than relying on the global financial system amid widespread sanctions or gold reserves held in potentially inaccessible foreign vaults.

The analyst described Bitcoin (BTC-USD) as a “multinarrative asset” that responds to various macroeconomic forces simultaneously.

While concerns about interest rates staying “higher for longer” weigh on the crypto industry, Gazmararian pointed to a countervailing force: governments may “start working the money printers again” to fund defense spending during wartime, potentially driving liquidity that would support crypto and other risk assets.

Despite the recent gains, Gazmararian cautioned against reading too much into Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) short-term performance. The asset remains down 45% from its highs at the end of last year, and she emphasized that broader market cycles are driving Bitcoin’s trajectory more than any short-term reaction to geopolitical uncertainty.

Gazmararian characterized Bitcoin (BTC-USD) as a “programmed asset” that has followed predictable four-year cycles since its inception.

She highlighted that institutional adoption has been “absolutely incredible in the last two years,” contributing to shallower drawdowns compared to previous cycles as mainstream investors enter the market.

Looking ahead, the analyst suggested Bitcoin (BTC-USD) could drop to around the $50,000 mark later this year if drawdowns reach 60%, or potentially test the low $40,000s if historical cycles fully repeat.

While infrastructure continues to mature, Gazmararian concluded that Bitcoin (BTC-USD) remains a “cyclical nascent market” whose price movements continue to reflect historical patterns.

Bitcoin-backed ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), and (EZBC).

data.title: DPC Dash reports FY results
data.description:
• Store-level EBITDA was RMB1,001.0 million, representing an increase of 20.4% from RMB831.4 million in FY2024. Store-level EBITDA margin was 18.6%, compared to 19.3% in FY2024.
    • Store-level operating profit was RMB739.7 million, representing an increase of 18.5% from RMB624.0 million in FY2024. Store-level operating profit margin was 13.7%, compared to 14.5% for FY2024.
    • Adjusted EBITDA was RMB634.6 million, representing an increase of 28.2% from RMB495.2 million in FY2024. Adjusted EBITDA margin was 11.8%, compared to 11.5% for FY2024.
    • Adjusted Net profit was RMB187.9 million, representing an increase of 43.3% from RMB131.2 million in FY2024. Adjusted Net Profit margin was 3.5%, compared to 3.0% for FY2024.
    • As of December 31, 2025, the Group held RMB1,001.5 million in cash and bank balances, as compared to RMB1,069.3 million as of December 31, 2024.
    • Total loyalty program membership was 35.6 million, representing an increase of 45.3% from 24.5 million in FY2024.
    • DPC Dash press release (DPCDF): FY Revenue of RMB5.38B.

data.title: Consumer sentiment in March falls more than initially anticipated
data.description:
Consumer Sentiment Index: 53.3 vs. 55.5 in preliminary reading and 56.6 in February, according to the University of Michigan Survey of Consumers' final March data.

One-year inflation expectations: +3.8% vs. +3.4% in preliminary and +3.4% prior.

Five-year implied inflation: +3.2% vs. +3.2% preliminary and +3.3% in February.

Current economic conditions: 55.8 vs. 57.8 preliminary and 56.6 prior.

Consumer expectations: 51.7 vs. 54.1 in preliminary and 56.6 prior.

Developing... Check back for updates.

data.title: Wall Street slides as Trump delays Iran energy strikes
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages traded lower on Friday as U.S. President Donald Trump extended the pause on attacks against Iran’s energy infrastructure into April.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -1.2%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.9%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.9% 

From a sector-by-sector standpoint, eight of the 11 S&P segments were in the red, with consumer discretionary at the bottom. At the other end of the spectrum, energy has been the strongest sector.

Treasury yields top multi-month highs. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) added 3 basis points to 4.45%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) dipped 3 basis points to 3.97%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) climbed 3 basis points to 4.97%. 

On the Middle East conflict front, President Trump described talks with Tehran as “going very well.” However, an Iranian official dismissed a reported U.S. proposal to end the conflict as “one-sided and unfair.” 

“The S&P 500 broke below 6,500 for the first time since this Iran war-driven pullback began, and that suggests that stocks want to go lower for the time being and we may very well be headed towards a 10% correction, which would leave us at 6,280. This may end up being one of the most attractive entry points for new money in the market since Liberation Day,” Glen Smith, chief investment officer of GDS Wealth Management, stated. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Entergy Corporation (ETR) climbed +4.7%, and shares of Palo Alto Networks (PANW) fell -7.3%. 

How do you think about gold as an investment? Share your opinion in our user poll.

data.title: Braskem plunges after reporting widening losses, going concern warning
data.description:
Braskem (BAK) down 11.4% in early trading Friday after reporting Q4 losses that nearly doubled from a year earlier and warning it faces "a material uncertainty" if it cannot address its rising debt.

Recent events "indicate the existence of a material uncertainty that may cast significant doubt on the company’s ability to continue as a going concern," Braskem (BAK) said.

The Brazilian petrochemical company said its Q4 2025 net loss attributable to shareholders nearly doubled to $10.28B reais (~$1.96B) from $5.64 reais in the year-ago quarter, on net revenue that fell 7% to $16.1B, due mostly to a tax writedown, provisions related to the Alagoas salt mine incident, and currency effects, rather than a collapse in the underlying operating business.

The company’s adjusted net debt increased to $7.5B in Q4 and leverage surged to 14.74x, while full-year recurring EBITDA plunged 45% to $3.15B reais from $5.76B a year earlier.

Braskem (BAK) has suffered through several difficult years, marked by repeated failed attempts by controlling shareholder Novonor to divest following its involvement in Brazil's Carwash corruption scandal about a decade ago.

The company may need to seek bankruptcy protection within the next few months, Valor Economico has reported, or pursue an out-of-court restructuring to address its rising debt.

data.title: Lm Funding America outlines 2026 production growth with record 782 petahash hash rate and focus on scaling
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: LM Funding America (LMFA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Bruce Rodgers, Chairman, CEO & President, emphasized that "2025 was a transformational year for LM Funding. We entered the year as an early-stage vertically integrated miner with a single-owned site and a modest Bitcoin treasury. We exited as a multisite vertically integrated platform with a significantly larger treasury and a simplified capital structure and the operational foundation to support the next phase of our growth." He highlighted the completion of the Oklahoma site ramp-up, the acquisition of the 11-megawatt Columbus, Mississippi facility, and the growth of Bitcoin holdings to over 356 Bitcoin by year-end.
  • Rodgers noted, "At December 31, our Bitcoin holdings were valued at approximately $31.2 million based on year-end Bitcoin prices, including the Bitcoin held by Galaxy compared to a market capitalization well below that level."
  • Rodgers stated the focus for 2026 is to "grow production, improve efficiency and increase Bitcoin per share," with immersion expansion in Oklahoma and steady operations in Mississippi providing the "platform to grow production, improve efficiency and increase Bitcoin per share."
  • Ryan Duran, President of US Digital & Mining Co and Vice President of Operations, reported that "we started with a single site in Oklahoma at approximately 560 petahash energized, and we exit with 2 wholly-owned sites, Oklahoma and Mississippi, totaling 26 megawatts of capacity and approximately 750 petahash energized across 22.5 megawatts at the end of the year, with further expansion continuing into Q1." He added that the new Mississippi site has "favorable power pricing of approximately $0.036 per kilowatt hour."
  • Richard Russell, Chief Financial Officer, stated, "For the fourth quarter 2025, total revenue was $2.4 million, up 8.7% sequentially from Q3 and up 19% year-over-year." He explained, "Mining margin for the quarter was 25% compared to 49% in Q3 2025. The sequential decline was driven primarily by a lower average Bitcoin price, which compressed revenue per coin against a relative fixed cost base."

Outlook

  • Rodgers projected, "As we move through 2026, our priorities are straightforward: grow production, improve efficiency and increase Bitcoin per share. We are already tracking toward record monthly production in early 2026, with February being our highest production month in company history."
  • Duran explained, "Looking ahead into 2026, we are now operating at record highs in energized hash rate, Bitcoin production and overall fleet efficiency, driven by our 2 vertically integrated sites with structurally low cost power."
  • The company continues to evaluate "accretive M&A opportunities in the 5 to 20-megawatt range, the same disciplined approach that led us to our Mississippi acquisition at roughly $355,000 per megawatt," according to Rodgers.

Financial Results

  • Russell reported, "Q4 production of 22 Bitcoin was up 25% sequentially from Q3, which partially offset the price-driven revenue compression."
  • He stated, "We reported a net loss of $18.2 million and a core EBITDA loss of $9.4 million for Q4 2025," later clarifying a reported net loss of $17.9 million and a core EBITDA loss of $9.3 million for the quarter. The net loss reflected "mark-to-market movements in our Bitcoin treasury as Bitcoin price declined from approximately $114,000 at September 30 to approximately $88,000 at December 31, producing an unrealized fair value adjustment of $7.8 million," as well as a noncash $5.4 million impairment loss on mining equipment.
  • As of February 28, 2026, Russell said, "we held 354.7 Bitcoins valued at approximately $23.8 million based on a Bitcoin price of approximately $67,000 or approximately $1.11 per share."

Q&A

  • Matthew Galinko, Maxim Group LLC, Research Division: Maybe firstly, will it take time to optimize production from the immersion-cooled units? Or are you kind of just right out of the gate where you want to be? Ryan Duran: Matt. Yes. So we -- right out of the gate, we're maxed out our 2 fog hash containers with S21 immersion miners that are the best we could get. So I guess in that context, yes, we are maxed out at roughly that 35 petahash per container as of right now and what's available on the market. And yes, I hope that answers your question.
  • Matthew Galinko, Maxim Group LLC, Research Division: And then as far as your pipeline for new site acquisition versus existing site expansion, can you maybe just go through how those 2 buckets look? Ryan Duran: Yes. So we're always on the hunt. We're always looking, keeping our finger on the pulse of what's out there. We are looking, as we've always maintained in that less than 20-megawatt range at ideally a power price in that $0.035 to $0.045 range. And that's what we target, whether it be existing sites or greenfields. And also, we have -- we also do -- our main focus as of right now, I'd say the easiest thing to point to is the additional a little over 3 megawatts that we have available at our current Mississippi site to continue building out. So that's already in our hands.
  • Matthew Galinko, Maxim Group LLC, Research Division: Maybe just reiterate how you think about funding new site acquisition and minor acquisition and how your current discount to NAV might influence how you think about capital spending and site acquisition and hash rate expansion? Richard Russell: That's a good question, Matt. It's got the same answer, but a moving target. We have to look at the dollar and then we look at projecting out as to where we want to be in terms of acquiring Bitcoin and holding Bitcoin when Bitcoin realizes price. So we put targets based on projections of where Bitcoin will be and then we sort of back into it. We're currently trading Bitcoin outside of the range where our forecasts were. So it makes some of our thinking more in the moment than using the long-range discipline. But it really boils down to if you spend $1 today, is it going to -- how much will it increase our Bitcoin holdings 5 years from now or 3 years from now. And that leans heavily towards both increasing the treasury and increasing the miners when it's at this price point. And then some of the mining opportunities are just timing based.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on operational optimization, expansion pipeline, and capital allocation, expressing a neutral tone with practical questions and no overt skepticism or strong concerns.
  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone in prepared remarks, frequently referencing operational progress, expansion, and ongoing strategic execution. In the Q&A, management remained transparent, addressing questions directly, though Russell acknowledged the challenges from price volatility, stating, "So it makes some of our thinking more in the moment than using the long-range discipline."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management retained a similar neutral-to-confident tone, though management's remarks now reflect more focus on scaling rather than foundational growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter shifted emphasis from foundational expansion and integration (Q3) to scaling operations and maximizing efficiency (Q4), with record hash rate and production levels reached.
  • Guidance language evolved from "improving per share value" to an explicit focus on "growing production, improving efficiency and increasing Bitcoin per share."
  • Analysts in both quarters concentrated on infrastructure development, future expansion, and capital decisions, but in Q4, questions focused more on operational optimization and funding strategies amid market volatility.
  • Key metric changes included an increase in Bitcoin production, expansion of energized hash rate, and a significant decrease in mining margin due to lower average Bitcoin prices.
  • Management's confidence remained steady but shifted from discussing building the operational base to emphasizing scaling and optimization.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited margin compression driven by declining average Bitcoin prices and an unrealized fair value adjustment of $7.8 million due to market price movements.
  • The company incurred a noncash $5.4 million impairment loss on mining equipment, and increased operating expenses from the full quarter integration of the Mississippi site.
  • Russell described liabilities of $22.4 million, primarily from the Galaxy Digital Master Digital currency loan and a short-term note payable, but indicated these are "manageable relative to our asset base."
  • Management is focused on closing the perceived disconnect between the market capitalization and the value of Bitcoin holdings and infrastructure.

Final Takeaway

LM Funding America concluded 2025 as a scaled, vertically integrated Bitcoin miner operating at record hash rate and production levels. The company more than doubled its Bitcoin holdings, expanded to two wholly owned sites, and launched immersion-cooled mining to improve efficiency. Despite margin compression from lower Bitcoin prices and increased costs from recent acquisitions, management remains focused on scaling production, maximizing efficiency, and closing the gap between equity value and net asset value through disciplined execution and active capital management.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Camden Property reports changes in senior management
data.description:
• Camden Property Trust (CPT) said it has promoted Alexander J. Jessett to CEO, joining the Board of Trust Managers, Laurie A. Baker to President and COO, and Benjamin D. Fraker to Executive VP, CFO, and Treasurer.
  • Richard J. Campo, Co-Founder, will serve as Executive Chairman of the Board, while Co-Founder D. Keith Oden remains Executive Vice Chairman.
  • Jessett joined in 1999, previously serving as President and CFO overseeing Finance, Capital Markets, Real Estate Investments, Construction, Accounting, Operations, and related teams.
  • Baker, also with the company since 1999, was Executive VP and COO, responsible for Property Operations, Strategic Services, and Sustainability.
  • Fraker joined in 2000, most recently Senior VP – Finance and Treasurer, managing Financial Planning & Analysis, Treasury, Tax, and supporting Capital Markets transactions.
